latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades — holding all four; the real action is tomorrow (JPM earnings + June inflation)
latest_messages.body: Quiet, slightly-red start. 10th straight scan with nothing clearing the buy bar — the fresh verifiable data starts tomorrow. Held all four (Lilly +8.8%, GE +8.5%, JPMorgan +2.2%, Deere −0.6%), all safety nets resting. Equity $101,509, down $312 (−0.31%) so far vs SPY −0.18% — roughly in line. ~44% cash ready. Tomorrow AM JPMorgan reports + June inflation prints — my green light to redeploy if something qualifies. Watching JPM for the +5% point where I upgrade its safety net to a trailing stop.

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market: no changes today — the big day is Tuesday (JPM earnings + June inflation)
latest_messages.body: Quiet Monday, no large-cap reports today, and stocks point slightly down into a two-event Tuesday. 10th straight scan with nothing clearing my quality bar — the fresh verifiable data starts this week. Holding all four (Lilly +10%, GE +9%, JPMorgan +2%, Deere flat), ~44% cash ready. Tomorrow AM JPMorgan reports and the June inflation number lands — my green light to redeploy cash if something qualifies. All four safety nets resting.

latest_messages.headline: Week ending Jul 10: −1.81% | SPY +1.35% | alpha −3.16% | grade C+
latest_messages.body: Second straight week trailing the market, and bigger than last week. New wrinkle: this time it wasn't just idle cash — two of our own 'steady' industrials fell (GE −4.8%, Deere −5.4%) while the S&P made new highs. Good news: no real money lost, just paper profit given back; every holding still green/flat, no safety net near firing. Our ~6-week lead over the market shrank from +6.9 to +2.2 points, so I'm flagging it. I'm holding buys until next week's JPMorgan/bank earnings + June inflation — the first fresh, verifiable data in weeks. If that passes and I still find nothing worth buying while the market climbs, I'll bring you a plain-English decision (deploy idle cash into a broad-market holding vs. stay cautious) rather than bend a rule myself.

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $101,813 · day −0.48% · SPY +0.45% · alpha −0.93%
latest_messages.body: Quiet, slightly-down day and we lagged a rising market — mostly because ~44% of the account sits in cash, which can't rise on a green day. No trades, all four safety nets resting, every thesis intact. This week we trailed the market by ~3.2 points (cash drag); holding fire until Tuesday's JPMorgan earnings + June inflation print to redeploy on verified data.
